attributes.content:
## Overview

The Licensing Agreement card gives the workspace a structured home for iP licensing terms: scope, royalties, territory, duration, and exclusivity.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Licensing Agreement acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Licensing Agreement can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Licensing Agreement can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Signature]]** -- Signature can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Licensing Agreement.
- **[[Alert]]** -- Alert can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Licensing Agreement.
- **[[Report]]** -- Licensing Agreement can feed rollups and summaries that surface trends, status, or outcomes.
- **[[Legal Contract]]** -- Legal Contract can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Licensing Agreement.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Licensing Agreement as the structured home for iP licensing terms: scope, royalties, territory, duration, and exclusivity and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Licensing Agreement when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Licensing Agreement stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
